summary.text: Title is vested of record in the parties identified in Schedule A; confirm marital or entity capacity against the vesting deed prior to closing.

citations.snippet: SAMPLE COMMITMENT FOR TITLE INSURANCE - Schedule A vesting

gaps: Estate or interest not stated in extraction.

summary.text: The legal description is not present in the extracted Schedule A; obtain a metes-and-bounds or platted description from the vesting deed before closing.
The chain of title is supported by recorded instruments; cite Deed Book and Page (and Instrument Number where present) for every link in the chain.

gaps: No chain-of-title links were extracted; back-title research is required.

summary.text: Identify each open lien with creditor, debtor, original amount, recording Book and Page, and current status; treat unreleased records as open until proven otherwise.

citations.snippet: mortgage to be insured must be signed, delivered and recorded

gaps: No open liens were extracted; confirm via lien search.

summary.text: No easements or restrictions appear in the extraction; verify against the recorded plat and any declarations of record.
Schedule B-I requirements must list payoff, release, execution, and recordation steps and identify the responsible party (seller, buyer, or lender).
Schedule B-II must enumerate standard and special exceptions and note whether each is removable upon survey or affidavit.
Confirm no delinquent property taxes via a current tax certificate, and order a survey to clear standard survey exceptions.

citations.snippet: title affidavit bring-down search tax exception

overall_summary.text: The commitment is curable: vesting, legal description, lien status, and tax/survey clearance require source-document confirmation before policy issuance.

open_questions_for_client: Confirm payoff of any open liens with Book and Page references.
